---
title: "Data Driven - Project"
author: "Nikita, Trilok, Deekshita"
date: "2024-10-10"
output: html_document
---
Reading the Dataset
```{r}
# Read the CSV file
HOU <- read.csv("C:/Users/dsrin/OneDrive/Desktop/MSBA/Data Driven/Data Driven Project/OUData.csv")
# View the first few rows of the dataset
head(HOU)
```
Understanding the Dataset
```{r}
# Structure of the data
str(HOU)

# Summary Statistics of the data
summary(HOU)

# Dimensions of the data
dim(HOU)
```
Changing the Data types to numeric
```{r}
#Setting the all the numeric variables as numeric
HOU$BloodPressureUpper <- as.numeric(as.character(HOU$BloodPressureUpper))

HOU$BloodPressureLower <- as.numeric(as.character(HOU$BloodPressureLower))

HOU$BloodPressureDiff <- as.numeric(as.character(HOU$BloodPressureDiff))

HOU$Pulse <- as.numeric(as.character(HOU$Pulse))

HOU$PulseOximetry <- as.numeric(as.character(HOU$PulseOximetry))

HOU$Respirations <- as.numeric(as.character(HOU$Respirations))

HOU$OU_LOS_hrs <- as.numeric(as.character(HOU$OU_LOS_hrs))

HOU$Temperature <- as.numeric(as.character(HOU$Temperature))
```
```{r}
#Factoring so as to represent the related groups
HOU$DRG01 <- factor(HOU$DRG01, 
                      levels=c(276, 428, 486, 558, 577, 578, 599, 780, 782, 786, 787, 789),
                      labels = c("Dehydration", "Congestive Heart Failure", "Pneumonia", "Colitis", "Pancreatitis", "GI Bleeding", "Urinary Tract Infection", "Syncope", "Edema", "Chest pain", "Nausea", "Abdominal pain"))
```
Exploratory Data Analysis
```{r}
#Checking for missing values and displaying
Missing = sum(is.na(HOU))
Missing
```
```{r}
# Mean Imputation for Numeric Columns
HOU_imputed <- HOU  # Make a copy of the data

numeric_vars <- sapply(HOU_imputed, is.numeric)  # Identify numeric columns
HOU_imputed[numeric_vars] <- lapply(HOU_imputed[numeric_vars], function(x) {
  ifelse(is.na(x), mean(x, na.rm = TRUE), x)
})

```

```{r}
#Checking for missing values and displaying
Missing = sum(is.na(HOU_imputed))
Missing
```
```{r}
# Load necessary libraries
library(ggplot2)
library(dplyr)
library(reshape2)  # For reshaping the correlation matrix for ggplot

# Select only numeric columns from the original dataset
numeric_data_with_outliers <- HOU %>%
  select_if(is.numeric)

# Calculate the correlation matrix
correlation_matrix_with_outliers <- cor(numeric_data_with_outliers, use = "complete.obs")

# Melt the correlation matrix into long format for ggplot
correlation_data_with_outliers <- melt(correlation_matrix_with_outliers)

# Create the heatmap
ggplot(data = correlation_data_with_outliers, aes(x = Var1, y = Var2, fill = value)) +
  geom_tile(color = "white") +
  scale_fill_gradient2(low = "blue", high = "red", mid = "white",
                       midpoint = 0, limit = c(-1, 1), space = "Lab",
                       name = "Correlation") +
  theme_minimal() + 
  labs(title = "Correlation Heatmap of Numeric Variables (With Outliers)") +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, vjust = 1, hjust = 1),
        axis.title.x = element_blank(),
        axis.title.y = element_blank())

```
```{r}
# Load necessary library
library(ggplot2)

# List of numeric variables to plot
numeric_vars <- c("Pulse", "BloodPressureUpper", "BloodPressureLower", "Temperature", "Respirations", 
                  "Age", "OU_LOS_hrs", "PulseOximetry", "BloodPressureDiff")

# Loop through each variable and create a box plot
for (var in numeric_vars) {
  print(
    ggplot(HOU, aes_string(y = var)) +
      geom_boxplot(fill = "skyblue", color = "black") +
      labs(title = paste("Boxplot of", var), y = var) +
      theme_minimal()
  )
}

```
```{r}
# Function to remove outliers based on IQR for a given column
remove_outliers <- function(data, column) {
  Q1 <- quantile(data[[column]], 0.25, na.rm = TRUE)
  Q3 <- quantile(data[[column]], 0.75, na.rm = TRUE)
  IQR <- Q3 - Q1
  lower_bound <- Q1 - 1.5 * IQR
  upper_bound <- Q3 + 1.5 * IQR
  data %>% filter(data[[column]] >= lower_bound & data[[column]] <= upper_bound)
}

# List of numeric variables for outlier removal
numeric_vars <- c("Pulse", "BloodPressureUpper", "BloodPressureLower", "Temperature", "Respirations", 
                  "Age", "OU_LOS_hrs", "PulseOximetry", "BloodPressureDiff", "Flipped")

# Apply outlier removal for each variable in the dataset
HOU_no_outliers <- HOU  # Start with the original dataset

for (var in numeric_vars) {
  HOU_no_outliers <- remove_outliers(HOU_no_outliers, var)
}

# Check the dimensions of the dataset after removing outliers
dim(HOU_no_outliers)

```
```{r}
# Load necessary library
library(ggplot2)

# List of numeric variables to plot
numeric_vars <- c("Pulse", "BloodPressureUpper", "BloodPressureLower", "Temperature", "Respirations", 
                  "Age", "OU_LOS_hrs", "PulseOximetry", "BloodPressureDiff")

# Loop through each variable and create a box plot
for (var in numeric_vars) {
  print(
    ggplot(HOU_no_outliers, aes_string(y = var)) +
      geom_boxplot(fill = "lightblue", color = "black") +
      labs(title = paste("Boxplot of", var, "(Outliers Removed)"), y = var) +
      theme_minimal()
  )
}

```

```{r}
ggplot(HOU_no_outliers, aes(x = Pulse)) +
  geom_histogram(binwidth = 5, fill = "skyblue", color = "black") +
  labs(title = "Histogram of Pulse", x = "Pulse", y = "Frequency") +
  theme_minimal()

```
```{r}
ggplot(HOU_no_outliers, aes(x = Age, y = OU_LOS_hrs)) +
  geom_bin2d() +
  scale_fill_gradient(low = "skyblue", high = "red") +
  labs(title = "Heatmap of Age vs Length of Stay", x = "Age", y = "Length of Stay (hrs)") +
  theme_minimal()

```


```{r}
# Load necessary library
library(ggplot2)

ggplot(HOU_no_outliers, aes(x = Pulse, y = OU_LOS_hrs)) + 
  geom_point(alpha = 0.6) + 
  theme_minimal() + 
  facet_wrap(~ DRG01) + 
  labs(title = "Pulse vs Length of Stay by Diagnosis (Faceted)", 
       x = "Pulse", y = "Length of Stay (hours)")

```

```{r}
# Load necessary library
library(dplyr)

# Create age groups
HOU_no_outliers <- HOU_no_outliers %>%
  mutate(AgeGroup = case_when(
    Age < 18 ~ "Child",
    Age >= 18 & Age < 35 ~ "Young Adult",
    Age >= 35 & Age < 55 ~ "Adult",
    Age >= 55 & Age < 75 ~ "Senior",
    Age >= 75 ~ "Elderly"
  ))

```
```{r}
# Load ggplot2 library
library(ggplot2)

# Box plot of Length of Stay by Age Group
ggplot(HOU_no_outliers, aes(x = AgeGroup, y = OU_LOS_hrs)) +
  geom_boxplot(fill = "skyblue", color = "black") +
  labs(title = "Length of Stay by Age Group", x = "Age Group", y = "Length of Stay (hrs)") +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1))

```
```{r}
# Perform one-way ANOVA
anova_result <- aov(OU_LOS_hrs ~ DRG01, data = HOU_no_outliers)

# Summary of the ANOVA results
summary(anova_result)

```
```{r}
# Tukey's HSD test for pairwise comparisons
tukey_result <- TukeyHSD(anova_result)

# Summary of Tukey's test results
print(tukey_result)

```

```{r}
# Perform one-way ANOVA
anova_result_age <- aov(OU_LOS_hrs ~ AgeGroup, data = HOU_no_outliers)

# Summary of the ANOVA results
summary(anova_result_age)

```
```{r}
# Tukey's HSD test for pairwise comparisons
tukey_result_age <- TukeyHSD(anova_result_age)

# Summary of Tukey's test results
print(tukey_result_age)

```
```{r}
# Set seed for reproducibility
set.seed(123)

# Define the training index (random sample of 70% of rows)
train_index <- sample(seq_len(nrow(HOU_no_outliers)), size = 0.7 * nrow(HOU_no_outliers))

# Split the dataset
train_data <- HOU_no_outliers[train_index, ]  # 70% for training
test_data <- HOU_no_outliers[-train_index, ]  # 30% for testing

# Check dimensions to confirm the split
cat("Training Set:", dim(train_data), "\n")
cat("Testing Set:", dim(test_data), "\n")

```
```{r}
# Run a logistic regression model
logistic_model <- glm(Flipped ~ Age + Pulse + BloodPressureUpper + PulseOximetry + BloodPressureLower + Temperature + Respirations + OU_LOS_hrs + DRG01,
                      data = train_data, 
                      family = binomial)

# Summary of the logistic regression model
summary(logistic_model)

```
```{r}
stepwise_model <- step(logistic_model, direction = "both")
```
```{r}
# Run the updated logistic regression model with significant variables
updated_logistic_model <- glm(Flipped ~ BloodPressureLower + Temperature + OU_LOS_hrs + DRG01,
                              data = train_data, 
                              family = binomial)

# Summary of the updated logistic regression model
summary(updated_logistic_model)

```

```{r}
# Load necessary libraries
library(pROC)
library(ggplot2)

# Predict probabilities on the test data using the updated logistic model
test_data$predicted_probs <- predict(updated_logistic_model, newdata = test_data, type = "response")

# Generate the ROC curve
roc_curve <- roc(test_data$Flipped, test_data$predicted_probs)

# Plot the ROC curve using ggplot2
ggplot(data = as.data.frame(roc_curve$sensitivities), aes(x = 1 - roc_curve$specificities, y = roc_curve$sensitivities)) +
  geom_line(color = "blue") +
  geom_abline(linetype = "dashed", color = "red") + # Diagonal line for random guessing
  labs(title = "ROC Curve for Logistic Regression Model",
       x = "False Positive Rate (1 - Specificity)",
       y = "True Positive Rate (Sensitivity)") +
  theme_minimal() +
  annotate("text", x = 0.75, y = 0.25, label = paste("AUC =", round(auc(roc_curve), 2)), color = "black")

```
```{r}
# Predict probabilities of Flipped = 1
predictions <- predict(updated_logistic_model, newdata = test_data, type = "response")

# Convert probabilities to binary outcomes (0 or 1) using a threshold (e.g., 0.5)
predicted_class <- ifelse(predictions > 0.5, 1, 0)
```

```{r}
# Confusion matrix
table(Predicted = predicted_class, Actual = test_data$Flipped)

# Alternatively, calculate accuracy, sensitivity, and specificity
library(caret)
confusionMatrix(as.factor(predicted_class), as.factor(test_data$Flipped))
```
```{r}
train_data$Flipped <- as.factor(train_data$Flipped)
test_data$Flipped <- as.factor(test_data$Flipped)
library(randomForest)
rf_model <- randomForest(Flipped ~ BloodPressureLower + Temperature + OU_LOS_hrs + DRG01, 
                         data = train_data, 
                         ntree = 500, 
                         mtry = 2, 
                         importance = TRUE)
print(rf_model)

varImpPlot(rf_model, type = 2)

```
```{r}
# Get prediction probabilities for the positive class
pred_probs <- predict(rf_model, newdata = test_data, type = "prob")[, 2]

# Generate the ROC curve
library(pROC)
roc_curve <- roc(test_data$Flipped, pred_probs)
plot(roc_curve, main = "ROC Curve for Random Forest Model")
```

```{r}

library(caret)

# Predict probabilities and convert to classes with threshold of 0.5
pred_probs <- predict(rf_model, newdata = test_data, type = "prob")[, 2]
pred_classes <- ifelse(pred_probs > 0.5, "1", "0")
pred_classes <- as.factor(pred_classes)

# Ensure that the levels of prediction and actual data are consistent
levels(pred_classes) <- levels(test_data$Flipped)

# Generate and print the confusion matrix
confusion_matrix <- confusionMatrix(pred_classes, test_data$Flipped, positive = "1")
print(confusion_matrix)

```
